task: Spike BLAS/OpenMP parallelization in hexa native C codegen path to accelerate CLM training matmul hot loops
verdict: GO — wrapper shim path working; direct extern path blocked behind hexa_v2 FFI ABI fix
option_tried: B (hybrid) — C wrapper stub exposing Hexa-FFI-shaped entry points, Hexa extern fn @link() calls into it

blas_speedup_matmul.vs_hexa_list_scalar_256x256: ~1457x (hexa array matmul was 0.358s vs BLAS 0.000246s — hexa lists add another 25x penalty over C-scalar from linked-list alloc churn)
blas_speedup_matmul.baseline_type: C-compiled -O3 triple-nested loop over raw f32 buffers (matches training/nn_core.hexa matmul math, minus hexa's list storage)

openmp_speedup_matmul.impl_notes: naive #pragma omp parallel for over outer loop, no cache tiling, no SIMD intrinsics — so plateau is bound by memory bandwidth, not compute. OpenBLAS is already multithreaded (already benefits from OMP internally) so the separate OMP kernel here is mainly a measurement control.

integration_effort.hexa_module: training/accelerate_blas.hexa (120 lines — extern decls + array↔raw helpers)

blockers_found.description: Live hexa_v2 binary FFI emitter in self/codegen_c2.hexa gen2_ffi_c_type maps both Float and float to C 'double'. Direct @link extern fn cblas_sgemm(... alpha: Float ...) produces a typedef that hits cblas_sgemm with double alpha/beta in d0/d1, but the real signature takes C float in s0/s1 — silent ABI mismatch on ARM64. Confirmed: direct extern cblas_sdot returned 0, direct extern cblas_sgemm returned C[0]=0.
blockers_found.workaround: C wrapper stub (hxblas_wrapper.c) presents ABI-compatible signatures (int64_t for ints/pointers, double for floats) and narrows to C float before calling real cblas_sgemm. Adds one function-call level of indirection, dwarfed by BLAS kernel time.
blockers_found.proper_fix_location: self/codegen_c2.hexa lines 493-504 gen2_ffi_c_type / gen2_ffi_c_ret_type. Add explicit 'f32' type that maps to C 'float' separate from 'Float' which maps to double. Then rebuild hexa_v2 via stage0 → hexa_cc.hexa → self/native/hexa_v2. Out-of-scope for this spike (agent #18 owns hexa_v2 rebuild).

blockers_found.description: hexa runtime hexa_ptr_write_f32(ptr, offset, val) uses offset as a BYTE offset, not element index. self/test_accelerate_ffi.hexa uses it as an element index (line 104: write_f32(buf, i, arr[i])) — that test would write 4-byte-wide values at byte offsets 0/1/2/3 and corrupt all but the first element. The test reports '(guarded)' for BLAS rows which effectively skips them on a fresh macOS host.
blockers_found.workaround: accelerate_blas.hexa helpers multiply by 4 explicitly — write_f32(buf, i * 4, arr[i]).
blockers_found.proper_fix_location: either rename runtime fn to ptr_write_f32_bytes, or add ptr_write_f32_elem(buf, i, val) that computes i*4 internally. Or fix test_accelerate_ffi.hexa to use byte offsets. Documented in training/accelerate_blas.hexa header.

blockers_found.description: Hexa array (list) type is immutable linked-list-like. Appending via 'a = a + [x]' is O(N) per op, building an N-element list is O(N²). In nn_core.hexa matmul, every call allocates tensor_zeros(M*N) via this path — the scalar matmul spends >95%% of wall time in list construction, not FLOPs. At 128² a BLAS matmul takes 0.08ms but converting A/B hexa lists to raw f32 buffers and back takes 1000+ms — 12000× worse.
blockers_found.workaround: Operate on raw f32 buffers end-to-end (alloc_raw / write_f32 / deref_f32). Never round-trip through hexa arrays in the inner loop.
blockers_found.proper_fix_location: refactor training/nn_core.hexa so Tensor is a raw f32 buffer (opaque integer handle) rather than a hexa array. Matmul, add, layernorm, softmax, attention all rewritten to use raw buffer ops. High-impact architectural change — should be its own task. Not strictly blocking, but without it BLAS acceleration is hidden behind an O(N²) list-copy moat.

dynamic_vs_static_ffi.current_path: dynamic — hexa_v2 emits hexa_ffi_dlopen(@link_path) + hexa_ffi_dlsym at main entry, builds function-pointer typedef, calls through it. Works.
dynamic_vs_static_ffi.static_path_exists_but_unused: self/codegen_c2.hexa gen2_static_ffi_block (lines 650-660) emits proper 'extern ret_type name(params);' declarations — but is NOT wired into the main codegen_c2() entry at line 32. Dead code only called from test_ffi_codegen.hexa. Wiring it would need compiler changes + rebuild.
dynamic_vs_static_ffi.static_path_upside: eliminates dlopen/dlsym path at startup, enables link-time symbol resolution, lets clang inline small calls, and avoids the float→double typedef bug (since gen2_static_c_type at line 548 correctly maps f32→float).
dynamic_vs_static_ffi.verdict: static FFI fix is the right long-term fix — both faster and correct. Dynamic shim is the short-term workaround.

deliverables.compiled_dylib_artifact: training/build/libhxblas.dylib (34 KiB, rebuild on every host)

toolchain.hexa_v2_compiler: /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/self/native/hexa_v2 (2026-04-15 21:35 build, do not rebuild here — agent #18 scope)
toolchain.libomp: /opt/homebrew/opt/libomp (21.1.8 / 22.1.1) — already installed, pulled in by openblas
toolchain.clang: Apple clang 17.0.0, arm64-apple-darwin24.6.0 — requires -Xpreprocessor -fopenmp syntax, not -fopenmp

next_action: Primary: Land this spike (commit wrapper + bench) so CLM hot paths have an immediate escape hatch. Rewrite training/nn_core.hexa matmul to check if a _USE_BLAS flag is set and delegate to acc_sgemm_raw for raw-buffer tensors. Gate behind a hexa constant so non-BLAS hosts still work via the existing scalar path.

Secondary: Track FFI_FLOAT_DOUBLE blocker in agent #18's hexa_v2 rebuild scope. Once the static FFI path is wired, migrate from @link('libhxblas.dylib') → @link('openblas') and drop the wrapper stub entirely. Expected time-to-resolve: one compile cycle after #18.

Major: Address HEXA_ARRAY_COPY_O_N2 — refactor nn_core.hexa Tensor type to raw f32 buffers. This is where the order-of-magnitude gains actually land. Without this step BLAS speedup stays hidden behind list-copy overhead on anything that talks to existing hexa-array code.
